source.derivation: Directly derived from the entry-level backend/software resume layout.
source.contentPolicy: Use this file as layout and writing-style reference only. Do not copy identity, company names, dates, or project claims unless separately supplied as candidate data.

page.density: compact early-career layout with balanced experience and projects

summary.heading: SUMMARY
summary.writingStyle: practical early-career engineer summary focused on backend APIs, automation, debugging, databases, cloud tools, and production-style workflows
avoid: too many buzzwords without implementation context

experience.heading: EXPERIENCE
experience.bulletCount: 6-8 for primary role, 5-6 for internship or secondary role

bulletWriting.pattern: Action verb + component/workflow + technology + practical outcome

technicalProjects.heading: TECHNICAL PROJECTS
technicalProjects.writingStyle: use projects to show depth beyond limited work experience; emphasize concrete systems, APIs, automation, testing, and debugging

skills.heading: SKILLS
skills.writingStyle: compact categories; JD-matching skills first; avoid listing unrelated tools

education.heading: EDUCATION
education.writingStyle: brief because entry-level layout gives more space to experience and projects

aiInstructions.layoutPriority: Use the entry-level section order and do not move education above experience unless the JD is explicitly internship/student-focused.
aiInstructions.contentGeneration: Generate entry-level content with credible implementation and support language.
aiInstructions.jdTailoring: Prioritize JD stack in summary, first role bullets, projects, and skills.
aiInstructions.outputRequirement: Return structured resume JSON matching this blueprint; do not return markdown.
